page: Amazon Web Services c8g.xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an arm64 architecture utilizing the custom AWS Neoverse-V2 processor. The instance provides 4 dedicated physical cores (4 vCPUs) running at 2.8 GHz, with no hyperthreading. Cache configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. There is no bundled local storage or GPU hardware. Baseline network bandwidth is rated at 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place the c8g.xlarge in the average tier for most operations. Single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests yield scores of 1707.19 and 6817.37 respectively, while Geekbench scores reach 1853.0 for single-core and 5899.0 for multi-core. Memory bandwidth is measured at 462.22 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 96.22 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The instance demonstrates strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression performance at 14.25 GB/sec, placing it in the top 25%. However, cryptographic performance is a notable tradeoff, with the Passmark CPU encryption test scoring in the bottom 10% at 750.07, despite hardware AES support.

Given its compute-focused 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated ARM-based cores, the c8g.xlarge offers a resource-efficient profile for workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. It is well-suited for static web serving, where it achieves a throughput of 9.92 GB/sec, and database operations, processing 9.33 million Redis SET operations per second. It can also handle lightweight text processing, software compilation, and small-scale language model inference, though text generation speeds drop significantly on larger models.
description: Amazon Web Services c8g.xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and a 2.8 GHz AWS Neoverse-V2 processor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores (4 vCPUs) with 36 MB of L3 cache, paired with 8.0 GB of system memory. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates average performance across most compute and memory tests, highlighted by a strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression speed of 14.25 GB/sec, though cryptographic performance is relatively weak. With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance delivers a cost-efficient profile for compute-bound workloads. It is highly suitable for static web serving, Redis database operations, software compilation, and lightweight text processing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated AWS Neoverse-V2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps under the Nitro hypervisor.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance with 4 dedicated AWS Neoverse-V2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage.
tagline: A dedicated arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring four Neoverse-V2 cores and a balanced memory profile for web serving and database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 4 dedicated AWS Neoverse-V2 arm64 cores running at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory and 36 MB of L3 cache
Delivers 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Optimized for static web serving, Redis operations, and software compilation
